items.title: Sustainability Reporting Season – 30 Jun 2026
items.desc: ASIC flagged six recurring compliance gaps in early mandatory climate reports. June 30, 2026 reporting season deadline applies to Group 2 entities. Ensure compliance with AASB S2 and Chapter 2M of the Corporations Act 2001.

items.title: IDR Data Reporting Deadline – 31 Aug 2026
items.desc: All IDR data reporting entities must submit complaints data for Jan–Jun 2026 by 31 August 2026. Submit via ASIC Regulatory Portal with sufficient time for validation and error correction.

items.title: Digital Assets Framework Commences – 9 Apr 2027
items.desc: Corporations Amendment (Digital Assets Framework) Act 2026 received Royal Assent 8 April 2026. DAP and TCP operators can lodge AFSL applications from month 12. Full compliance due by October 2027.

items.title: ASIC 2026 Enforcement Priorities – Private Credit & Financial Reporting
items.desc: ASIC doubled investigations and court proceedings. New focus areas include private credit practices, financial reporting misconduct (especially unlisted entities), insurance claims handling, and misleading pricing.

items.title: FFSP AFSL Exemptions – Transition by 31 Mar 2027
items.desc: Treasury Laws Amendment Act 2026 provides new AFSL exemptions for FFSPs including comparable regulator, professional investor, and market maker exemptions. Transitional relief expires 31 March 2027.

items.title: ASIC Fees Increase – 1 Jul 2026
items.desc: From 1 July 2026, ASIC fees have changed following CPI increase. Review impacted fees and update budgets accordingly.

items.title: Tranche 2 Entities Obligations Commence – 1 Jul 2026
items.desc: AML/CTF obligations commence for lawyers, accountants, real estate agents and jewellers. Must enrol with AUSTRAC by 29 Jul 2026. Requires documented AML/CTF program, CDD and suspicious matter reporting.

items.title: AML/CTF Program Reforms – 31 Mar 2026
items.desc: Reformed AML/CTF Act commenced 31 March 2026. Existing reporting entities must implement unified, outcomes-focused programs replacing Part A/B structure. Ongoing CDD obligations apply to all customers.

items.title: Compliance Officer Notification – 30 May 2026
items.desc: Entities enrolled on 30 March 2026 must notify AUSTRAC of AML/CTF compliance officer by 30 May 2026. Newly regulated businesses have until 29 July 2026 or 14 days after enrolment.

items.title: New TTR and SMR Reporting Forms – 1 Jul 2026
items.desc: AUSTRAC releasing new threshold transaction report and suspicious matter report forms from 1 July 2026. Existing entities can transition to new forms anytime until 30 March 2029.

items.title: AUSTRAC 2026 Risk Snapshots – AI and Virtual Assets
items.desc: AUSTRAC released Money Laundering, Terrorism Financing and Proliferation Financing updates on 12 May 2026. Highlights growing AI/crypto threats. Critical input for risk-based AML/CTF frameworks.

items.title: AML/CTF Amendment Bill 2026 – AUSTRAC Powers
items.desc: New bill gives AUSTRAC CEO powers to restrict or prohibit high-risk products, services or delivery channels (e.g. crypto ATMs). Amendment to 'financing of terrorism' definition also proposed.

items.title: AER Separates from ACCC – 1 Jul 2026
items.desc: Competition and Consumer Amendment (Australian Energy Regulator Separation) Act 2025 takes effect. AER becomes standalone Commonwealth entity with own staffing and budgetary autonomy.

items.title: Electricity Retail Code Obligations – 1 Jul 2026
items.desc: AER and ACCC joint compliance bulletin issued. Retailers must comply with new Electricity Retail Code obligations effective 1 July 2026. Enhanced reporting on concessions and rebates required.

items.title: Embedded Network Family Violence Policies – 1 Jul 2026
items.desc: Condition 27 of Retail Exempt Selling Guideline requires most exempt sellers in residential/mixed settings to implement dedicated family violence policies by 1 July 2026.

items.title: 2026 Rate of Return Instrument Review
items.desc: AER conducting RORI review with final decision expected December 2026. Determines allowed return on capital for regulated electricity and gas networks for next regulatory cycle.

items.title: Future of Network Regulation Paper – Jun 2026
items.desc: AER released Information Paper on 19 June 2026 examining how electricity network regulation may evolve in response to changing energy system and decentralisation.

items.title: Exemptions Guidelines 2026 Review – Consultation Open
items.desc: AER initiated review on 18 May 2026 to align exemptions framework with flexible trading reforms. Proposes reduced regulatory burden for commercial embedded network retrofits.
